# Warmth Engine Observatory

> A Coordination Intelligence platform systematically tracking AI infrastructure coordination dynamics across geopolitical blocs through verified events, typed Coordination Connections, and Sovereign Capability Profiles.

The corpus is built for machine consumers and served via an MCP server (JSON-RPC 2.0) and a REST API. Every classification traces to a published, DOI-registered methodology. Evidence-based classification from primary sources with post-implementation verification — observable facts over inferred meaning.

## What WEO tracks

Events are classified through a five-stage pipeline by three orthogonal systems — Event Type (Coordination, Infrastructure, Policy, Corporate), Tier (Cross-Bloc, Between-Bloc, Intra-Bloc), and Domain (Energy/Infrastructure, Security, Technology, Trade) — with two analytical enrichment layers: Industry Tags and Environmental Nexus Tags. The Warmth Engine Ratio (WER) measures the balance between documented environmental resource demand (ENT-1) and documented environmental governance (ENT-5) across applicable events.

Coordination Connections across seven defined types — including policy cascades, parallel policies, and funding flows — map documented relationships between events at three confidence levels (CC-V Verified, CC-E Established, CC-A Analytical).

Sovereign Capability Profiles assess state actors across seven AI infrastructure dimensions through a three-gate designation framework producing four designations: Principal AI Actor (PAA), AI Infrastructure Keystone (AIK), Advanced Capability State (ACS), and Participant.

## Machine access

- [MCP server](https://warmthengine.com/mcp): 15 read-only tools over the corpus — events, Coordination Connections (CCs), Sovereign Capability Profile (SCP) actors, coordination topology, and methodology lookup. JSON-RPC 2.0 over streamable-HTTP. A human-readable tool reference (descriptions + input schemas) is served at the same URL when fetched with Accept: text/html.
- [REST API](https://warmthengine.com/api/): read-only JSON over the corpus. Key endpoints: /api/events, /api/connections, /api/actors, /api/scp, /api/topology, /api/threads, /api/blocs, /api/health.
- Full-tier access: send an access token as an `Authorization: Bearer weo_...` header on the MCP or REST endpoints; tokens are obtained by subscription — see /support.

## Methodology (peer-citable, Zenodo)

- [WEO Methodology Manual](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18427565)
- [WEO Methodology Rationale](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18427582)
- [Towards Coordination Science (TCS)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18427584)
- [Tier-Crossing Dynamics (TCD)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18427586)

## Site

- [Atlas](https://warmthengine.com/atlas.html): network, timeline, and connectedness views of the coordination corpus with guided journeys across coordination, material, and stack substrates
- [Events](https://warmthengine.com/events.html): verified event database with classification, enrichment layers, and Coordination Connection links
- [Profiles](https://warmthengine.com/profiles/): Sovereign Capability Profile assessments with dimension scoring and Dimension Watch
- [Bloc register](https://warmthengine.com/blocs.html): geopolitical bloc classifications underpinning the tier system
- [Research](https://warmthengine.com/research.html): independent theoretical research and methodology extensions (Open Access, Zenodo)
- [Methodology](https://warmthengine.com/methodology.html): five-stage pipeline overview with Manual and Rationale access
- [Support](https://warmthengine.com/support.html): access tiers and pricing
- [warmthengine.com](https://warmthengine.com): main site
